meta.title: Mercedes-Benz OM646/OM647 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost

hero.h1: Mercedes-Benz OM646/OM647 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
hero.subHeadline: The Mercedes OM646 and OM647 are durable 2.1-litre four-cylinder and 2.7-litre five-cylinder diesels — robust workhorses, but injector seal leaks and turbo wear are well-documented. Backed by real UK enquiries from EnginesMarket.co.uk.

rows.metric: Confidence: 85% confidence — based on verified specialist rebuild reports and UK workshop consensus.

rows.value: Mostly cheap, well-understood repairs (injector seals, EGR, turbo)

rows.metric: Available but specialist-order only; Mercedes parts premium applies

rows.value: Recon cost 30–44% of lowest host-vehicle value

One-line verdict: The OM646 and OM647 are rugged, high-mileage diesels when maintained — but 'Black Death' injector seal leaks and turbo wear are common past 100,000 miles — buy only with documented service history.

rows.metric: Best for: Buyers wanting a durable, economical Mercedes diesel with proven commercial-grade longevity.
rows.value: Avoid if: You're looking at a high-mileage example with no injector seal or turbo service records.

rows.metric: Labour estimate: Fitting an OM646 or OM647 typically takes 8–12 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer).
rows.value: → Compare OM646/OM647 engine prices from UK specialists
1. How much does an OM646/OM647 engine replacement cost?

rows.metric: A reconditioned OM646 or OM647 typically costs £2,000–£3,500 supply only, varying by specific variant and application. Fitting adds £800–£1,200 depending on labour rates. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.
rows.value: 2. Is the Mercedes OM646/OM647 a reliable engine?

rows.metric: The OM646 and OM647 are rated 3.5★ out of 5 — they are widely regarded as durable, high-mileage workhorses. The OM646 in particular is known for exceptional longevity, with examples exceeding 500,000 km. However, injector seal leaks ("Black Death") and turbocharger wear are well-documented from around 100,000 miles.
rows.value: 3. What is the most common OM646/OM647 failure?

rows.metric: The most common issue is injector seal failure — the copper washer between the injector and cylinder head fails, allowing combustion gases to escape, causing carbon buildup ("Black Death"). Turbocharger wear from oil residue in the charge air system is also common from 150,000–180,000 km.
rows.value: 4. Which Mercedes models use the OM646 and OM647?

rows.metric: The OM646 powered C-Class (W203/W204), E-Class (W211), CLK (C209), Sprinter, Viano and Vito as 200 CDI and 220 CDI models. The OM647 — a 2.7-litre five-cylinder — was fitted to E 270 CDI, ML 270 CDI, G 270 CDI, and S 270 CDI models.
rows.value: 5. How long does the OM646/OM647 last?

rows.metric: With proper maintenance — regular oil changes, injector seal inspections, and DPF-friendly driving — both engines are capable of 200,000–500,000 miles. The OM646 is considered one of Mercedes' most durable diesel four-cylinders.
